title: Freeze mission-level ADC requirements
summary: Approved the baseline requirement set for a 12-bit, 5 MSPS radiation-hardened SAR ADC with guaranteed operation through 100 krad total ionizing dose and single-event upset recovery within two conversion cycles.
impact: Requirements document locked for architecture trade study.
Verification team aligned coverage model to the 12-bit transfer function and SEE recovery goals.

title: Adopt segmented capacitor DAC with bootstrapped sampling switch
summary: Selected the segmented capacitor DAC architecture and a bootstrapped front-end switch after comparing energy, linearity, and Radiation Hardening margin against charge-redistribution alternatives.
impact: Analog schematic freeze targeted for March.
Physical design reserved centroid-aware placement area for the capacitor array.

title: Use buffered 1.0 V ADC Reference Voltage architecture
summary: Initial decision to use a buffered 1.0 V ADC Reference Voltage generator to maximize dynamic range during early analog bring-up.
impact: Bandgap and buffer design entered detailed simulation.
ATE team started drafting trim hooks for the reference monitor path.

title: Proceed with Packaging Revision B using ceramic CQFP assembly
summary: Approved Packaging Revision B with the existing ceramic CQFP stack-up to preserve the pilot schedule while prototype assembly data remained nominal.
impact: Mechanical drawings released to the packaging house.
Reliability plan assumed 48-hour Burn-In Testing for the pilot lot.

title: Accept reduced metal density fill near capacitor array
summary: Accepted a reduced local density fill strategy around the capacitor array to preserve matching, with explicit monitoring of Lithography Alignment sensitivity during signoff.
impact: Tapeout checklist gained an added lithography hotspot review.
Yield team prepared a dedicated split for overlay monitor correlation.

title: Replace buffered 1.0 V reference with trimmed 0.9 V reference path
summary: Revised D-003 after radiation drift simulations and hot-corner headroom analysis showed the buffered 1.0 V ADC Reference Voltage path would not hold settling margin after total dose.
impact: Analog team inserted trim range expansion and removed one gain stage from the reference buffer.
Verification updated mixed-signal regressions for new trim tables.

title: Cancel Packaging Revision B and move to Packaging Revision C
summary: Cancelled D-004 after reliability failures showed lid attach delamination and bond sweep during Burn-In Testing. Packaging Revision C adds an alternate attach compound and tighter wire loop rule.
impact: Pilot assembly repeated with revised package materials.
Production readiness gate shifted to depend on fresh Reliability Qualification data.

title: Extend Burn-In Testing from 48 hours to 96 hours at 135C
summary: Replaced the pilot screen assumption from D-004 with a longer stress screen after latent package and reference settling drifts were only visible beyond 60 hours.
impact: ATE capacity was rebalanced to keep sample throughput acceptable.
Reliability Qualification exit criteria updated for the production readiness review.

title: Tighten lithography-aware capacitor matching rules and restore density fill
summary: Revised D-005 after first silicon Yield Loss analysis linked DNL excursions to overlay drift near the array edge. Additional dummy fill and stricter Lithography Alignment checks were mandated.
impact: Metal fill cookbook updated for the metal-spin contingency.
Future rad-hard derivative projects will inherit the new matching rule deck.

title: Reduce scan compression ratio to improve at-speed diagnostic visibility
summary: Replaced the earlier high-compression test plan after diagnosis data from the pilot lot proved insufficient to isolate intermittent digital control faults seen during Reliability Qualification.
impact: Pattern count increased, but diagnosis and failure triage improved significantly.
Production test documentation updated before readiness signoff.
